title: oxalis pes-caprae var. pes-caprae
intro: Oxalis pes-caprae var. pes-caprae, commonly known as the Bermuda buttercup, is a type of flowering plant in the wood sorrel family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, and is believed to have a range of health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. The leaves and flowers of the Bermuda buttercup can be used to make herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments. Additionally, the plant's leaves and flowers have been used in culinary applications, adding a sour taste and flavor to salads and other dishes.
